info.title: BKey API
info.description: Biometric approval infrastructure for AI agents — OAuth 2.1 + CIBA

bearerAuth.description: EdDSA-signed JWT obtained via OAuth 2.1 client_credentials or CIBA

merchantApiKey.description: Merchant API key issued during merchant onboarding. Distinct from OAuth JWT.

get.description: Returns the JSON Web Key Set for verifying BKey-issued JWTs (EdDSA / Ed25519).

post.description: RFC 6749 token endpoint. Supports grant types: client_credentials, refresh_token, urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code, urn:openid:params:grant-type:ciba. Request body must be application/x-www-form-urlencoded.

id_token.description: OIDC ID token (if openid scope was granted)

post.description: Initiate a CIBA backchannel authentication request. The user receives a push notification to approve on their mobile device; poll POST /oauth/token with grant_type urn:openid:params:grant-type:ciba to retrieve the approval token.
RFC 7009 token revocation. Revokes an access or refresh token. Always returns 200 (per spec) even if the token is unknown, to prevent token enumeration.

RFC 8628 device authorization request. Returns a user_code and verification_uri that the user visits on another device to approve. Poll POST /oauth/token with grant_type urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code to retrieve the token.

get.description: OIDC UserInfo. Returns the authenticated subject (user DID) and granted scopes for the bearer token. Use this to verify a CIBA approval token and identify which user approved the action.

sub.description: Subject identifier — user DID or client ID

get.description: OpenID Connect discovery document. Lists all supported endpoints, grant types, scopes, and signing algorithms.
Fetches the mobile device's X25519 public key for end-to-end encrypting vault payloads. Requires the vault:store scope. The mobile app must have registered a key first.

publicKey.description: Base64-encoded X25519 public key of the mobile device

keyId.description: Optional identifier for the public key

404.description: No vault public key registered yet

schema.description: No vault public key registered yet

post.description: Agent requests access to a vault item by name. Sends a push notification to the user for biometric approval. Poll GET /v1/vault/access/{id} for the status + decrypted payload.

id.description: Access request ID — use this to poll status

challengeHex.description: Challenge the mobile device signs on approval

get.description: Poll the status of a vault access request. Returns status "pending" until the user approves on their mobile device. When status becomes "approved", the e2eeCiphertext field contains the base64-encoded encrypted payload.

status.description: One of: pending, approved, denied, expired, consumed

e2eeCiphertext.description: Base64-encoded encrypted payload. Present (non-null) only on the first poll after approval, when status transitions to "consumed". Null otherwise.

post.description: Agent initiates a checkout (purchase). Sends a push notification to the user for biometric approval of the payment amount + merchant. When invoked with a CIBA-approved token (approve:payment scope), the payment is auto-approved without a second push.

status.description: Initial status — "pending" for standard flow, "approved" when created with a CIBA auto-approve token

get.description: Poll checkout status. Returns status "pending" until the user approves on mobile. When status becomes "approved" and later "completed", the orderConfirmation fields will be populated.

status.description: One of: pending, approved, rejected, completed, expired

get.description: Initiates a merchant onboarding handshake. The merchant provides their store URL and a return URL; BKey validates the request and redirects the merchant to an approval page. On success the caller receives a 302 redirect.

302.description: Redirect to the BKey approval page. The client must follow the Location header to complete the merchant onboarding handshake.

Location.description: URL of the BKey approval page the merchant should be redirected to.

400.description: Invalid request: the store_url or return_url failed validation.

schema.description: Invalid store_url, unsupported platform, or SSRF rejection

post.description: Create a payment session (merchant-facing). Called by plugins (WooCommerce, Medusa, etc.) to start a BKey-approved payment flow. The merchant authenticates with a mk_live_* / mk_test_* API key. Returns an approvalUrl to redirect the shopper to.

status.description: One of: pending, approved, completed, rejected, expired, refunded

approvalUrl.description: URL the shopper visits to approve payment

price.description: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g., cents)

401.description: Missing or invalid merchant API key

schema.description: Missing or invalid merchant API key

post.description: Create a payment session (agent-facing). Used when an AI agent initiates a purchase on behalf of the user. When called with a CIBA-approved token (approve:payment scope), the session is auto-approved.

status.description: One of: pending, approved, completed, rejected, expired, refunded

approvalUrl.description: URL the shopper visits to approve payment

price.description: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g., cents)

get.description: Get a payment session (public polling endpoint). Returns non-sensitive fields only — used by the shopper approval page to display payment details and poll for status changes.

status.description: One of: pending, approved, completed, rejected, expired, refunded

approvalUrl.description: URL the shopper visits to approve payment

price.description: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g., cents)

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: OAuth 2.1 token, device auth, CIBA, revocation

tags.name: Vault
tags.description: Encrypted secret storage with biometric access

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: Payment methods and shared payment tokens
